name: Shadow AI and Unsanctioned Data Flows

mappings.notes: Shadow AI tools are external AI capabilities the adversary may already operate or compromise
Unapproved AI services may themselves be adversary-controlled or compromised
Sensitive data pasted into shadow AI tools ends up in external data stores accessible to the AI service provider
Acceptable use policy for AI tools — approved list, prohibited use cases, data handling requirements
Impact assessment of shadow AI on organisational data security posture — ungoverned data flows quantified
Evaluation of shadow AI detection effectiveness — DLP coverage, endpoint monitoring
Response procedures for detected shadow AI usage — data impact assessment, user notification

mappings.control_id: Shadow AI usage is a foreseeable risk requiring mitigation in the risk management system
mappings.notes: Ungoverned AI tool usage mapped and treated in Art. 9 risk assessment

mappings.control_id: Organisations deploying AI are responsible for AI used within their operations
mappings.notes: Shadow AI use by employees does not exempt the deployer from AI Act obligations triggered by that use

mappings.control_id: Deployers must use AI systems as instructed and ensure appropriate oversight
mappings.notes: Deployers have affirmative obligations to prevent and detect shadow AI usage
Policy explicitly covering acceptable use of AI tools — approved list and prohibited use cases
Due diligence and security requirements for any cloud-based AI service including shadow AI SaaS
DLP controls detecting and blocking sensitive data transfer to unapproved AI endpoints
AI acceptable use policy as a formal ISMS policy document

Foundational
Hardening
Foundational

mappings.control_name: V1.1.4 — Trust boundaries documented and enforced
V7.2.2 — Log entries contain required information

mappings.notes: Network controls block OT data flows to unapproved AI endpoints — DLP at OT DMZ boundary
Approved AI tools only on OT workstations — unapproved services blocked at network layer
Shadow AI discovery triggers incident response — data impact assessment, vendor notification
OT data confidentiality requirements apply to training data
Assess sensitivity of OT data in training sets
Monitor for unexpected exfiltration of OT training data
Acceptable use policy for AI tools — approved list, prohibited use cases, data handling requirements
Users trained on shadow AI risk — policy awareness, prohibited tool use, reporting obligations
Monitoring for use of unauthorised software — shadow AI tool usage detected on endpoints and network
All approved AI tools managed as suppliers — TPSP assessment, contractual obligations

mappings.control_id: Acceptable use procedures for AI tools — approved list, prohibited use cases, employee acknowledgement
Shadow AI vendors not in vendor risk programme — shadow AI discovery identifies unapproved vendors
Shadow AI usage identified as a risk in assessment — ungoverned data flows, training use by vendors
Confidentiality policy covers AI tool use — employees informed of restrictions on sharing confidential data

mappings.notes: Shadow AI tools processing CHD are unapproved TPSPs — not in TPSP list, no written agreements
Shadow AI discovery as part of TPSP monitoring — identify unapproved vendors receiving CHD
Network controls prevent CHD flows to unapproved AI endpoints — block at network layer
PCI security awareness programme covers shadow AI risk — staff trained on prohibition
Acceptable use policy covers all GenAI services — unapproved use is a policy violation; AI asset register maintained
Network-level detection of data flows to unapproved AI endpoints — DLP covering SaaS AI API calls
All AI services assessed before authorisation — vendor security review, data processing agreement, sub-processor disclosure
Egress filtering to block unapproved AI service domains; proxy logging of AI API calls
Require data classification check before any dataset enters training pipeline
Policy mandating PII/sensitive data removal before training
Automated PII scanning of all training datasets before use
Automated tests verifying PII detection coverage
Periodic probing of deployed models for sensitive data memorisation
Foundational
Foundational
Foundational

mappings.control_id: Pipeline service account with access to sensitive data stores
mappings.notes: Scope pipeline credentials to approved data sources only

mappings.control_id: Credentials embedded in config files include sensitive data source access
mappings.notes: Audit config files for embedded credentials

mappings.control_name: Reuse existing well-secured software — AI tool vetting
mappings.notes: Vet all AI tools, services, and plugins before organisational adoption; verify data handling, security posture, and compliance capabilities

mappings.control_name: Protect all code from unauthorised access — shadow AI detection
mappings.notes: Implement controls to detect and prevent unauthorised AI tool usage that processes organisational data outside governed pipelines

mappings.control_name: Identify and confirm vulnerabilities — shadow AI exposure monitoring
mappings.notes: Establish procedures to identify data exposure from shadow AI tool usage; define triage and remediation workflows for unsanctioned data processing

mappings.control_name: Least Functionality — approved AI tools only
mappings.notes: Restrict AI tool usage to approved, vetted tools within the FedRAMP boundary; disable or block access to unauthorised AI services

mappings.control_name: External Information System Services — third-party AI controls
mappings.notes: Require FedRAMP authorisation or equivalent for all third-party AI services; block data transfer to unauthorised AI tools

mappings.control_name: Access Enforcement — AI tool access control
mappings.notes: Enforce access control preventing data transfer to unauthorised AI tools; monitor and block shadow AI usage

mappings.control_name: Third-Party Risk — AI tool vendor oversight
mappings.notes: Include AI tools and services in third-party ICT risk management; conduct due diligence before approval; monitor ongoing compliance with financial sector requirements
Identify and register all AI tools — authorised and unauthorised — in the ICT asset inventory; maintain discovery mechanisms for unapproved AI services

mappings.control_name: ICT Risk Management — AI tool governance
mappings.notes: Define approved AI tools policy in ICT risk management framework; require vetting and approval before use in financial data processing

incidents.name: Samsung employees leak source code and meeting notes via ChatGPT
